sections.paragraph:
ἐνωτίζομαι 1 aor. ἐνωτισάμην LXX, 2 pl. ἠνωτίσασθε Jer 44:8 to listen carefully to what is said, give ear, pay attention to (s. οὖς 2; Hesych.; Doukas 84, 19 IBekker [1834]) τὶ someth. (Syntipas p. 12, 6 al.; Psellus p. 77, 6; Gen 4:23; Job 33:1; TestReub 1:5, Iss 1:1) τὰ ῥήματα Ac 2:14 (cp. Ps 5:2). Abs. B 9:3 (Is 1:2).—On the form, s. B-D-F §123, 2.—M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pl. = plural

Hesych Hesych , V a.d.—List 5

Doukas Doukas , Byz. hist., XV a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Syntipas Syntipas , a collection of fables under the name of Syntapis; before XI a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Psellus Psellus , History, XI a.d.—List 5

TestReub TestReub = Testament of Reuben, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 343
